display_title: 1985-05_06 — BEARING 315 Newsletter of Cascade Orienteering Club May/Jun 1985
body_html:
Page 1

BEARING 315
Newsletter of Cascade Orienteering Club
May/Jun 1985

NOTICE - Change in Schedule of Events
I have already started to butcher the schedule of events that was published in the Jan/Feb issue of "Bearing 315". My apologies for any inconvenience this may cause, but it is a tentative schedule! In looking at this issue's schedule, you will notice a change in locations for several meets. First of all, the canoe "O" meet scheduled for May 19 had to be rescheduled since also on May 19 will be the "Beat the Bridge" run which starts and ends at Husky Stadium. (Since 10K runners have an hour to get out and back before the Montlake drawbridge is raised for boat traffic, there is usually a huge turnout. - Ed.) After all, we orienteers prefer going through massive wilds rather than through wild masses. So in place of the canoe-O we moved up the Hamlin Park meet to make it more convenient to potential new members north of us. And since we have a new map we might as well start using it, therefore Lynndale took Hamlin Park's place on June 1.

I wish I could say there will not be any more changes to the years schedule, but ... it is best to check the latest newsletter or call me for the latest meet info. Oh, by the way, I'm looking at August when it should be nice out for rescheduling the canoe-O meet.

  • John Huth

Class in Lynnwood
We have a new map! Bud Keith was instrumental in obtaining the base map for Lynndale Park, Lynnwood a few months ago, and since that time, Eileen Williams and Mike Schuh have been busy in the field making the new Orienteering map. Many thanks also to John Huth for this fine cartography work.

City of Lynnwood is sponsoring a class currently being taught at Lynndale by Eileen on Sunday mornings, and will also provide funding when the final color edition is printed. The terrain is an interesting mix of open fields and wooded area, with many trails and contours. We look forward to having a club meet there soon.

  • Eileen Williams

(For info about the class, phone Eileen at 524-1831. The club meet will be on Sat, June 1 according to the revised schedule. -Ed.)

NOTICE - Meet Fees Increase
Club President Debbie Newell wants all members to be informed that the Board of Directors approved an increase of 1.00 per registration per meet. There have been no increases for 4 years but costs have risen, particularly the cost to make new maps and the cost to replace lost/strayed/stolen control markers. So, 3.50 for non-members and 2.50 for members on courses using multi-color maps, and b&w 3.00 for non-members and 2.00 for members, will be the new rates, with cap for families.

Page 2

Officers & Board Members
The officers and executive board members of the Cascade Orienteering Club are:

PositionNamePhone
PresidentDebbie Newell488-7471
Vice PresidentJohn Huth522-1304
SecretaryValerie Tinsley822-6254

Promotions
Car Pools

BEARING 315 contains results, a schedule, articles of interest contributed by members or selected from other sources and lots of material written by the editor, Harvey Friedman; letters to the editor are most welcome. Any correspondence to Cascade Orienteering Club or to this Newsletter should go to

P.O. Box 31375
Seattle, Wash. 98103

Other clubs wishing to receive BEARING 315 in exchange should send their newsletters directly to the above address.

Next Issue
All material to be published in the next issue must be received by 15 June, the earlier, the better.

Club dues
Please check the date on the mailing label to see when your membership expires. Renew promptly if you want to continue receiving BEARING 315, the club newsletter, and seeing how your name might be misspelled in the results column. An application form is enclosed somewhere in this newsletter for your convenience. Words of both thanks and welcome go to the following new or renewing club members: Scott Coble, the L. C. Darveau family, Shari del Moral, Jessica Hirschfelder, Matt Marinkovich.

Pot-O'-luck
The next Cascade O.C. potluck will be at Bruce McAlister's at 14014-81st Pl. N.E. in Bothell at 6:30 on 10 May. Phone 821-6862. As usually occurs at these potlucks, the food should again be excellent and you have the opportunity to socialize and talk about something other than orienteering ( or you can talk about orienteering ). After that one, the next potluck will be Friday, 5 July in Woodland Park; see event schedule for details.

Page 3

RESULTS

Volunteer Park Bunny-O
6 April 1985

Many thanks to Kelly Hawkins for working registration, Erin Renshaw for setting the novice course and working the finish, Michelle Everett for setting the intermediate and advanced courses as well as picking up the controls, field checking, and take care of start times, ( and for helping to fix my flat tire), and a wowie, zowie thanx to Jessica Hirschfelder for everything from course design, to finish times, to the exterior decorating of this meet. Appreciation is also noted to John Huth for his guidance concerning this event.

Page 5

Letters to the Editor

To the Editor,

Please extend our thanks to WIOL Coordinator Dan Waugh and all the other people who helped with the winter high school league. The Wilson students enjoyed the chance to learn a new sport and have something interesting and challenging to do every Saturday. They were impressed with the competitors from the other schools and from the amount of volunteer work it takes to put on these meets.

In spite of things like climbing trees, losing a compass in the snow, running off the map, numerous DQs for miss-punching, they are ready to try again next year.

Sincerely,

Carl Moore
Wilson High

Carl Moore is the coach of the Wilson High team that won the state championships. Perhaps one of the reasons that they won was that Coach Moore himself enjoys orienteering and participates as often as he can.

From Gary McCausland of Nisqually Orienteers comes the word that unless we are informed to the contrary, all Nisqually meets have registration between 10-11 am and not 10-12 as we in Cascade O.C. normally have. Also, the contact person for Nisqually meets in Tacoma is Mary Forbes 584-6401.

Report on last club meeting
The last meeting of COC on Monday, 4 March 1985 at the University Branch of the Seattle Public Library was comprised of a two part program and a surprise announcement. For all those who wondered why they were asked to bring a compass to the meeting, Sandy Kish Miller and Robert Miller led a most enjoyable exercise on figuring out both how far it was between two points on a map and also how many paces it would. They prepared maps with scales of from 1:3000 to 1:32000 to keep us thinking. Since each of these maps were used by at least one person in the room, we also had a contest to see who could identify them all. No one could, perhaps because Robert and Sandy couldn't agree themselves on one of them. Anyway, it was a good introduction to figuring distances for the less experienced club members in attendance.

Following that, Harvey Friedman went over the available meets in Europe, Canada, the U.S., and the rest of the world during spring and summer. This rundown was much drier than the first part of the meeting.

Finally, an emotional Jan Vrana announced that he would have to live in Germany for a few months because his mother in Czechoslovakia was ill.

Page 6

SCHEDULE OF EVENTS

GENERAL INFORMATION: Unless otherwise noted, novice, intermediate and advanced courses are available at all club meets. Normal registration and start times are from 10:00 AM to NOON and entry fee is usually $2.50 for members and $3.50 for non-members. Regional and National (Class 'B' and Class 'A') events may have a higher entry fee depending upon sanctions and awards given to winners in various categories. Instruction for beginners is always available at all events. A limited number of compasses are available on a rental basis at registration.

Lets have a good showing to let the Lynnwood Parks & Rec. people know about the interest in the sport. John 522-1304

Page 7

DateEvent/LocationDetails
Sun, 9 JuneCentennial Park, Victoria, B.C.1 pm, 604-479-9519 Dick, Diane
Sat/Sun, 15-16 June"Fourth Annual Snowmelt Meet"Oldenburg Lake (south of Willamette Pass) Sat. 12-2, Sun. 9-12 $5. Jeff 503-245-4725
Sun, 16 JuneWonderwood Park, LaceyGary 352-5542
Sat, 22 JuneLincoln Park, north of Vashon Ferry Terminal, West Seattle1:5000, color. We have waterfront property reserved for this year's meet, so hope for a sunny, clear day. For those that don't get their fill of craziness on the course can take a dip in the sound! John 522-1304
Wed, 26 JuneMystery evening meet6-8 pm, contact Dave Enger at 822-6254 for location and more information
Fri, 5 JulyWoodland Park Team Relay and Potluck, SeattleW. Greenlake and Greenlake Way N., shelter #5, 1:5000 color. 5 pm til ?? We will be gathering at the 2nd shelter south of the lawn bowling area. Bring a dish to share and be prepared for a dusk team relay course and campfire to tell some tall "O" tales. See next issue of newsletter for more details.

Cascade Chatter
Yes, indeed. There was a 2 page article on orienteering in the April issue of 'Sunset' magazine. We've already received 1 enquiry as a result of it. Anyone who wants to read it but doesn't want to buy the magazine, see Harvey....2 members of Nisqually Orineteers and at least 1 Cascade member are going down to the Bay Area for the CLASS A meet 18-19 May. Good luck to Gary, Nathan, and Roger and anyone else who might be going...Another big event of the same weekend is the impending wedding of Dave Enger and Valerie Tinsley. We wish them all the best...Mark down 16 August on your calendars if you don't want to miss out on the pot-O-luck at Knut Olson's Gold Creek Trout Farm. In addition to whatever other food we might bring, we also get the opportunity to catch and cook fresh trout. See you there....The count stands at 6 adults and at least 2 juniors for this year's Swedish 5-day at Falun. 4 of us will continue on to the Scottish 6-day near Pitlochry. Such a turnout sets a record for participation by Cascode O. C. members in European meets....We received a letter from Ingemar who is now living and working for NCR in Oslo. He has been doing lots of cross-country skiing and ski-O in the wonderful winter sports areas around Oslo, and presently plans no O for summer. Any one wishing to write to him can write to Ingemar Jansson, Bentsebrugata 29B, N-0469 Oslo 4, Norway and his work phone # is (02)-490095 for anyone wanting to talk with him. He figures it will be at least a year before he gets back to the Seattle
(continued on page 8)

Page 8

area....Several members of the Roosevelt High School Orienteering Team have been accepted by MIT, but haven't decided yet if they attend that fine school. Good luck wherever you go, and don't stop orienteering.... By the time you read this, Anne and Harvey will be living in a different house, 4 blocks west of Robert & Sandy. We dread having to move the 10 years accumulation.
